_note: Hand-picked food and drink anchors for Northern Belle guests with a 5h 10m Edinburgh window. Skewed toward quick, characterful bites and cocktails (a sumptuous six-course dinner is waiting on the return journey, so nothing heavy). All within an easy walk of Waverley.

places.name: The Devil's Advocate
places.blurb: A converted Victorian pump house tucked down a close off the Royal Mile. Edinburgh's finest small-plates-and-whisky stop, two minutes off the train.

places.name: Ondine
places.blurb: Roy Brett's Michelin-recommended seafood room. The two-course express lunch is one of the best deals in town if you have a small window between shows.

places.name: The Bow Bar
places.blurb: Tiny no-fuss whisky bar on Victoria Street with over 300 single malts. The proper Edinburgh dram experience, no cocktail menu, no nonsense.

places.name: Hawksmoor Edinburgh
places.blurb: Three minutes from Waverley, in the old India Buildings banking hall. Order the Shaky Pete's Ginger Brew at the bar, a proper Fringe ritual.

places.name: The Voodoo Rooms
places.blurb: Lavish first-floor cocktail rooms above the Café Royal, hosting Fringe music and cabaret in the same building. A complete pre-train wind-down.

places.name: Mary's Milk Bar
places.blurb: Hand-churned gelato made daily from a tiny Grassmarket counter. The five-minute treat that doubles as a Fringe-day reset.
